segment-text: DreamWorks
A GARAGE
stands alone. Separated from the house.
Still in the night.

Until... BLACK SMOKE slowly billows up from the four-square pane of windows. More and more of it by the moment. The windows SHATTER outward and give way to FLAMES that crawl up the outer wall. A FIRE... the fire. And as the fire consumes the garage...
HOTEL - NIGHT

It's night. A father and his son walk alongside of the large pool that illuminates the grounds. The father, BRIAN BURKE, mid 30s, displays all the signs of a man settling into fatherhood; benign smile, expanding midsection and aging wardrobe. His son, DORY, six years old, owns big Jolly-rancher green eyes, a small nose, puffy cheeks and a built-in innocence. The air is summer night. They hold hands as they slowly walk on a little bridge that crosses over the water. As Dory stops and points down to the pool.
The water is green, Daddy. Brian stops. They stand on the bridge.
No, it's florescent. DORY What's flor... es... cent?
Florescent means lit from with-in.
Am I florescent, Daddy?
And Brian regards his child. Holds the moment. Knows it's as fleeting as it is special. Dory swings back and forth, waiting for the answer. Brian draws in a deep breath, then...
Yes, Dory, you are.
As Dory smiles up toward his father...
EXT. HOTEL/PARKING LOT - DAY

Titles and music. Brian and his wife Audrey pack up their minivan. Their daughter, HARPER, ten years old, swings her little brother around and around until Audrey tells them to get into the minivan. Kids in the van, luggage in the back, Audrey finally faces her husband and sighs. He gives her a smile. She returns it by holding up the keys and dangling them. He grabs the keys and kisses her. sleep. All told without a word.
EXT.
He'll drive so she can
SHOOTING GALLERY/PARKING LOT - DAY
Titles and music continue. A dirty parking lot behind a boarded up building. The ground is littered with beer cans, cigarette butts and needles. Find a body. His name is DWAYNE, was Dwayne.
He overdosed A girl who calls herself SPRING, in her twenties, white-girl dreads, tattoos and pierces, nods in and out... on her third opiate rush of the day.
(louder) Dwayne.

No reaction. She studies him. Knows the score right away. Spring finds her feet and walks over to him. Checks his pulse. Spring doesn't cry or freak out or anything... she simply reaches into his pocket and removes six crumpled up dollar bills. A few junkies look over... but they don't do a thing. And before she goes, Spring looks around the body, looking for something specific... and she finds it. AN ENVELOPE already opened with a big red stamp on it. The stamp has a caricature on it with the caption under it... "Cream." This is the brand of heroin they were just using. As Spring pockets the money, the envelope and goes... EXT.
THE 5 FREEWAY - AFTERNOON
The minivan glides through the Washington landscape. Summer green and Northwest fresh. As the minivan races toward Seattle. INT.
MINIVAN - AFTERNOON
Music and titles continue. Harper attempts to teach her little brother a clapping rhyme.
Itsy bitsy, let's get busy, up the well and down the shoot, climb the tree...

Dory is no good at this. Clapping out of rhythm and screwing up the words. He simply breaks into laughter and gives up. Brian drives as Audrey plaintively looks out the open window. Brian regards her, his wife of eleven years...
As the heavy dusk-laden wind blows through her long and flowing hair. Thirty-six years old with an accessible beauty
kind of beauty that threatens but the kind that invites. Childlike and giving. Audrey catches Brian admiring her and she smiles. And it's a smile that explodes... every time. INT.
SOUTH SEATTLE RECYCLING CENTER - NIGHT

Music and titles winding down. We're at weighs station for homeless and addicts. A center where they trade collected cans for money. Find... JERRY SUNBORNE In his late thirties, Jerry's rail-thin with rock star hair. He's very good looking -- heroin chic without the chic... and too much of the heroin. His eyes used to pierce with sensuality but they haven't in a long time... dulled by the years of opiate abuse. Behind the counter, Jerry hands a homeless woman a few bills for her cans. She smiles at him toothless and heads on her way. Jerry shouts across the center to his boss.
Ray, I'm out.
Alright, Jerry, take it slow.
Music and titles die down as Jerry pulls away from the window and heads on his way. Spring catches his stride.
When?
Like an hour ago.
Jerry stops and faces her.
Is he dead?
She confirms with a simple nod.
I told him to find me if he had any problems.
Well... he didn't.
And she hands Jerry the envelope.
And you're not everyone's father, Jer.
Were you with him? No.
I found him after...
As Jerry looks at the envelope.
You're lying.
She rolls her eyes as he tosses aside the envelope. "Cream."
Looks like Zoey's.
As they continue to walk...
Fuckin' Dwayne. Yeah...
SPRING SEATTLE'S MADISON PARK NEIGHBORHOOD - NIGHT

An upper class neighborhood of Seattle with hills and views of Lake Madison. Brian pulls the minivan into the driveway of a fantastic contemporary home made of wood and glass. He looks in the backseat where both kids are sleeping. Then looks over to Audrey who is also asleep. He nudges her. She slowly wakes up and looks around. We home?
AUDREY
Brian confirms and points to the kids. She looks over her shoulder at both of them sound asleep on each other.
Don't let them grow up.
She looks to him with a smile.
I'm going to make us pasta. I'm going find those cheesy bed trays your sister gave us so we can eat upstairs. And we'll open the wine we just bought.
She grabs his hand and looks into his eyes.
And our vacation won't end till tomorrow.
But he looks away. Resolute.
I'll clear the van when I get back. Losing her smile...
Not now, Brian. We just got back from vacation. I have to.
In their quiet "kids are sleeping" voices.
Why do you have to?
Because it's his birthday.
Audrey leans forward, now very upset. Louder.
You were just there.
He looks at her. An impasse.
You should've seen him as a kid, Audrey, I can't explain it, he was special, he was... It trails off.
Everytime you go there I'm terrified you won't come back. And frustrated...
Stop being so dramatic.
Stop being so naive.
She stews. He stews. In stalemate.
It's his birthday, Audrey...
Brian's anger has now somehow risen.
Who the hell knows how many more he's even going to have?
As she shakes her head in an unhappy capitulation then turns around to see... both kids -- now wide awake.
SOUTH SEATTLE APARTMENT - LATER
The Cream envelope is open on the table. Spring's out. on the couch with a syringe and a tie at her feet.
Asleep

Jerry watches an Anthony Robbin's "Awake The Giant With-In" infomercial. Warm on Cream. A knock on the door. Jerry doesn't hear it. Another knock. Louder. Jerry snaps out of it and slowly rises. Jerry opens the door to see...
Happy Birthday, Buddy.
Jerry gives him a hug. Opens the door.
Is it August third?
Brian heads in, sees Spring asleep on the couch. Sees the syringe then quickly looks up to Jerry. Coffee?
Clearly in the warmth of the opiate... Yeah.
Cool.
MINIVAN - NIGHT
Wearing his old tattered suit jacket, Jerry gets in the passengers seat and sees the junk in the back.
We just went to Victoria.
Jerry is sitting on something... he leans up to find himself holding Dory's stuffed dinosaur.
Victoria. Remember when we went up there with your Dad?
Jerry looks the dinosaur straight in the eyes. Brian looks over and smiles.
(re: dinosaur) Dory insists he lives in the minivan... won't bring it inside the house because he's a carnivor.
Jerry smiles but it quickly washes away with a heavy sigh.
I'm Thirty-seven now... I'm tired,
While staring into dinosaur's plastic black eyes...
And I feel older than this guy.
IZZY'S DINER - NIGHT
Brian drinks a coffee, Jerry inhales an ice cream.
Audrey angry you came down to my spot at night? No.
Jerry smiles... they both know it's a lie.
Yes. I just don't know why you have always been so against seeing her again.
I am not seeing anyone until I am clean.
She'd be crazy for you. are crazy for you.
All women
We're not fifteen anymore, Brian. Anyway, she doesn't want to see me. (MORE) JERRY (CONTINUED) I provide nothing. I am just a poisonous threat.
Brian accepts that.
That's not true.
And Jerry just smiles. It is true and they both know it.
Look, Brian, you have to see her point.
You always take her side.
As Jerry lights up a smoke.
... and you do provide.
Name one thing.
Brian thinks about it for a beat. Then with a smile...
Always taking her side. As Jerry sucks in a long draw, then...
The Fed raised rates. going to hurt you? Is that
No, people will still buy, this refi boom's got good legs. Good.
And Brian just looks at him.
QUICK STOP SUPERMARKET - NIGHT
Brian wheels a little cart down the aisle of this no frills mini-mart. Jerry at his side.
I appreciate this. Audrey would kill me if I didn't grab this shit.
No worries...

It's just so hard to find the time to get anything done. With the kids... and the insurance from the fire... and we're building an office for me next to the garage... I'm not happy with the work, I think I might have to fire the workers.
Brian stops, noticing his complaints are trivial. And that makes him a little embarrassed. So he deflects it with...
And Audrey's fucking someone else.
Jerry laughs.
Checking to see if I'm still listening?
No, you haven't seen her. There's no way a woman that looks that good could only fuck me.
Jerry laughs even harder. This is their game.
Our neighbor, Landon Hicks, he's this really rugged mountain-man type. I don't think he talks... (concluding) She's in love with Landon Hicks.
You're probably right. But you know -- gotta take the good with the bad -- at least you're still in the rotation. Brian agrees... and now they're both laughing...
SOUTH SEATTLE - NIGHT
As Brian and Jerry carry the grocery bags down the street.
It's just like Senior year with Emily Rotham. She was the hottest girl in school and she dug you.
She dug you.
At first. But after we all started hanging out, she got more interested in you. And she told me then I told you... and you didn't do anything about it.
That was a long time ago. A SKETCHY CHARACTER takes an interest in them.
little nervous. Jerry's not.
Don't worry. We're fine. (and then) I'm going to tell you now what I told you then and I've been telling you ever since. Accept the good... because they're going to make you accept the bad.
I hear you.

Your wife loves you. You know that. You have this amazing family and the most incredible woman in the world who loves you, my man. You have everything any man would want. Accept the good. You have what I'll never-And it tails off.
I know, I know. This is stupid, you don't need to listen to this.
Fuck that! I do need to listen to this. You're my only real friend left in the world, do you know that?
Jerry's a little animated. He's very emotional as he's coming out from under the warmth of the opiate.
They're all gone, Brian. I know it's my own fault, but they're all gone.
And Jerry stops walking. on. Turns to Brian. Looks him straight
You're a success. You're an amazing father and an amazing husband.
I hear you.
And I know you don't have to come out here... at night... to see me. Jerry--
No, let me finish. I appreciate it more than you know. It's the only time in my day where... it's just that... well, thank you.
Jerry's holding back an emotional rush due to his inceptive withdrawal. If he doesn't get high soon, it'll turn to irritable then to sickness then to pure hell.
JERRY'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
They stand at the doorway. Okay. Brian readies to leave.
I'm off.
A little hug.
Thanks for coming by, bro.
Brian breaks. Looks at Jerry.
Happy birthday.
Jerry sharply looks away...
Oxymoron...
And then back at Brian.
But I appreciate the sentiment.

And Brian goes. Jerry heads to the couch and flops down on it. Packs a bowl, lights up and draws. And as Jerry tokes, he notices... the grocery bags... everything Brian just bought at the store... sitting against the wall. Jerry can only shake his head and smile as he moves to the window... down to the street below... where his best friend jumps into a minivan and drives off.
INT. BURKE HOUSE/BESIDE THE GARAGE - NIGHT

An empty space. The lights turn on and Brian stands in the doorway. Looking over the room. Some painting supplies litter the center but most of the work is done. Brian steps in and takes a deep breath, surveys the room. And he stands there for the longest of moments. INT. Still.
BRIAN AND AUDREY'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
Brian comes into the bedroom to find Audrey and Harper in the bed watching television.
It's way past her bedtime.
And they're both crying...
Oh no -- you're not watching that Lifetime movie again.
It's this commercial we just saw. Beyond belief...
You're crying over a commercial?
Do you know what they do to baby seals in Alaska?
They were so cute, Dad. You have to give them a lot of money.
And Audrey looks up to him and holds up a written-on notepad.
They have a website where we can make a donation -- I wrote it down.
Brian just rolls his eyes.
Harper, out.
Harper kisses her mother good night then gets out the bed. As she passes her father...
Seal-hater.
Audrey turns off the tv.
Brian gets ready for bed.
He's all fucked up, Audrey. (and then) They forgot to put dimmers on the lights.
What's it matter?
It's important. I told them I wanted dimmers. I'm firing them and going with the Polish guys.
He gets into bed, turns off the lights. Stares at the ceiling. She rolls over into his hold... eyes now closed.
You're taking the kids swimming at Howard's tomorrow.
Dory's putting his head under.
Don't pressure him too much.
Brian grunts in acknowledgement. His mind elsewhere. A long silence, she falls back asleep... he can't. He starts laughing... she tussles awake. What?
I told him you were fucking Landon Hicks. Brian!
I was kidding. Don't worry, he knows I was kidding.
And now Audrey starts laughing...
If I were sleeping with anyone it sure as hell wouldn't be Landon Hicks. He doesn't talk.
Who would it be?
She thinks for a beat, then...
The water guy.
The water guy?
He reminds me of a cowboy.
The guy that delivers the Arrowhead water reminds you of a cowboy?

She confirms through her laughter. After they quiet, Audrey lays her head on his chest. He throws his leg over her knees. She grabs his arm so it's now draped around her with his hand resting on the back of her neck.
Brian continues to stare at his ceiling. and furrows it under her hair. Do it... She grabs his hand
And he sighs then runs his hand up and down the back of her neck. She loves this. Then...
His head's going under tomorrow... even if I have to push it.
Audrey lets out a little laugh.
Oh, that's nice.
And they lay there. Her head on his chest, his leg sprawled over her knees... his hand running the nape of her neck. INT.
INDOOR POOL - DAY
A glass enclosed indoor pool. across the way.
An impressive home can be seen
Harper, Brian and Dory are all in the shallow end. Dory stands on the steps with the water going to his little chest. Audrey sits at the edge, getting her feet wet.
One... two... three...
Harper and her father both put their head under. Dory stands still... terrified.
I don't want to.
Brian and Harper come up. HOWARD GLASSMAN, kind round man of forty-five and the Burke's neighbor, heads over.
You need anything, Brian? Howard owns this house.
I'm fine, Howard, thanks. coming in?
Howard's wife, DORIS, a very stiff woman dressed in full equestrian gear chimes in.
He doesn't swim. Knowing where this is going... Okay...
Who spends two hundred thousand dollars on a swimming pool and doesn't swim?
Thank you, Doris -- that's enough.
Next I think he'll want to build a Mosque.
Funny, Dear...
Doris rolls her eyes and walks off.
I'm going for my ride.
Howard shrugs apologetically to Brian who looks back to his son...
Don't you want Cold Stone? No.
Dory, I'm right here, nothing is going to happen. It's like the shower, you get wet in the shower.
No, I don't.
He's being difficult for the attention, Dad.
Thanks, Dr. Freud. Go touch the drain or something.
Harper swims off to the deep end.
Dory, just try. I did try.
No, you didn't. Look, everybody has to face their fears... it's a fact of life. You need to do this, now you're six years old... it's time.
And he starts to cry.
No! I don't want to. make me.
You can't Brian's losing his patience.
Actually I can.
As Audrey lowers herself into the pool.
Honey, are you kidding?
Audrey grabs a big red plastic ball. Yes.
(to Dory) Go to Mommy. I'm going to swim with your sister.
As she heads toward her crying son.
I know. I know. Your father is a very abusive man.
She throws the ball at him. It bounces off his head. And Audrey takes her son into her hold... who's now laughing. EXT.
BURKE HOUSE/PATIO - EVENING
The Burkes eat their dinner.
C'mon, what about chicken, Harper? You can eat chicken.
Chickens are animals, Dad. eat animals.
I don't
Since when?
Since meat is murder.
(to Harper) Where'd you learn that? Dad...
I know things. Not everything I know was learned. Dad...
Actually, it was learned, Harper... that's the nature of knowledge.
Eating her dinner, Audrey looks up to Brian.
Did you take the sixty dollars out of the minivan, baby? What?
I had three twenties in the ashtray. They're gone.
I didn't take them.
Asks...
Dad... What Dory?
I like meat.
I know you do.
Audrey quietly eats. And never to look up from her food...
Was your friend in the van the other night? Brian stop eating.
He didn't take it.
Now looking up and locking in his stare.
Of course he did. Who?
He wouldn't.
Who didn't take the money?
Audrey stands and clears the dishes.
(frustrated) Wake up, Brian.
Mom -- who?
Audrey doesn't answer as she moves to the sink and starts washing the dishes. Harper walks by Brian, he snags her and throws her onto his lap. She giggles. Dad.
Stop it...
Meat is so murder.
(laughing) Well, it is... who took the money?
Brian looks into his daughter's big eyes, smiles and says... Nobody.
BURKE HOUSE/AUDREY'S STUDIO - EVENING

The basement is a make-shift restoration studio. A canvas sheet covers the floor. Old pieces of stripped furniture line the wall. Audrey surveys a half-finished sideboard in the middle of the room. Brian appears from behind and wraps him arms around her. She just nods. She doesn't want to fight so they stand there. Looking at the piece.
The walnut stain wasn't a good choice. I totally fucked this up.
No you didn't. It looks great.
I can't get an honest opinion out of you. You love everything I do. (upset, churning aloud) I hand-strip it, I remove the old finish from top to bottom, I even out color tones, I check the
glides. I steam. I sand. I detail. I stain. He takes in a big whiff of her hair, nuzzles her neck.
And after all of that... all of that work... (a beat) I only have one chance at the finish. Because if I screw that up -- it kills the whole thing.
Whispering into her ear...
I'm just here to deliver the water...
She laughs...

And he turns her around, kisses her. Long and deep. She kisses him back. They begin to heat up. She pushes him back against the wall, kissing him, untucking his shirt. He grabs her hair, tugs it back, exposes her neck, kisses it. She grinds up against him. Reaches for his pants, unbuttons the top... but... from the kitchen... Dad...
Aroused and breathless...
I told them I'd get Coldstone.
And she's a little frustrated, turned on. But she steps back and collects herself. Breaking into a smile. Which he returns with...
To be continued.
EXT. SEATTLE HEIGHTS/PARKING LOT - NIGHT
Brian heads out of the Cold Stone holding the ice cream. As he walks toward the minivan, he notices something across the way. A MAN leaning up against a van, breathing heavily, distressed.
Are you okay?
The man doesn't answer. Brian notices blood on the man's hands as he walks toward him. Excuse me?
Are you--
And Brian stops... sees something on the ground.
lying in a pool of blood -- severely beaten. Jesus.
And the man collects his breath and shouts out. Fuck! DISTRESSED MAN As he kicks her... very hard. Brian runs over.
She lets out a squeal.
The man then bangs his fist into the side of the van. Hard. The man totally unhinged. The woman barely conscious. Jesus!
She's my wife.
She's dying.
The man is hysterical. Crying and pounding the van. DISTRESSED MAN
You're going to kill her.
DISTRESSED MAN
She needs help.
DISTRESSED MAN
She needs help. I'm going to call an ambulance. She lied.
DISTRESSED MAN Brian takes out his cellular and dials 911. He waits.
She broke my fucking heart.
And Brian's calm -- doesn't want to upset this man anymore than he is.
Jesus, damn it, pick up... (to the distressed man) Take a deep breath, you need to calm down.
The man has a seat on the ground, throws his head in his hands and weeps.
I love her!
And Brian notices... this distressed man is holding something in his bloody hand... what he used to beat her down... a .22 Caliber handgun.
Count down from ten.
I... I... Ten... nine...
We're close on Brian now as he speaks into the phone. We can hear the man counting down -- on six.
Yes, I need an ambulance at South West Vista avenue and Elm... there is a woman here who is badly beaten.
And off screen the man stops counting after four. Looking down at the woman, Brian talks into the phone.
It's domestic. Her husband is very upset, he's going to need medical assistance as well.
Brian doesn't ask for police or indict the man at all, he
Brian's mind races, trying to remain calm. see the man is no longer on the ground. AND BRIAN BURKE And he turns to Close on... As he looks up...
You love her!

As his face washes white... Don't! And Brian dives forward as we hear a LOUD... CRASH Audrey jumps back with a start. She's wrapped up in a towel, in her bathroom with the bath running. The crash came from downstairs from the...
KITCHEN
Harper stands over a broken glass, trying to pick up the large chards. Audrey heads in with her towel wrapped around herself.
Don't clean it up, baby, you'll cut yourself.
I poured too much.
It's fine. Wait till your Dad
comes home and he'll clean it up.
He's been gone like an hour.
You know how Cold Stone gets. I'm taking my bath. Just stay away from the glass.
Harper does and smiles over to her mom.
Can I watch "Desperate Housewives" tonight?
You're looking at one right now, Harp.
Harper shakes her head in disapproval at the stupid joke.
Pleaaaase, with you and Dad?
As Audrey heads on her way back to her bath.
We'll see. Don't let your brother go near the glass.
BURKE HOUSE/LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
Audrey comes into the room. Her hair is wet. Dory plaintively looks out the window.
You look like a little doggy
waiting for his owner, Dory.
(whining) Where's my ice cream, Mom?
Audrey grabs the phone. As she dials...
I don't know... (into phone) Where are you? Why aren't you on your phone, baby?
COLD STONE CREAMERY - NIGHT
A mad house on a summer night. Families buzzing everywhere. Assembly-line ice cream. The clerk picks up the phone.
Cold Stone Creamery... (listens, then...) I don't know, Mam, it's so busy in here tonight. Sure...
She holds the phone away and shouts.
Is there a Brian Burke in here? Brian Burke? As the clerk waits for a response... the flashing cherry
illuminations from the ambulances and police cars outside
roll across her face.
EXT. COLD STONE CREAMERY - SAME
As the police tape off the crime scene. lie on the ground. Three dead bodies
BURKE HOUSE - NIGHT

A wind sweeps across the front lawn. A boot steps down then stops. The boot belongs to a POLICE OFFICER. He stands facing the house, staring it down. His hand begins to shake uncontrollably and we realize... he's terrified. And he draws in a deep breath. And he Looks down to his hand and it stills. As he exhales. Finally, he shares a look with his PARTNER and both officer head for the door... passing Dory's toys and Harper's bike on the way.
INT. BURKE HOUSE/LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
Audrey's on the phone.
I'm sure it's nothing but could you just come over and watch them for like ten minutes while I go over to Cold Stone.
The doorbell RINGS. OFFICER outside. Audrey looks to see a uniformed POLICE And she immediately knows. And she vacantly drops the phone.
EXT. BURKE HOUSE - NIGHT
Audrey opens the door. The POLICE OFFICER is shaking, horrified... has never done this before and he's young.
Are you Mrs. Brian Burke?
Audrey nods sharply -- swallowing her world.
May we come in? No.
Your husband was in an accident, a domestic issue and he was shot, Mam.
She's not crying or shaking or anything.
He's dead, Mrs. Burke. He... was trying to help... he took a bullet. Something else is taking over.
Pure shock. Cold determination.
What am I supposed to tell my children?
I'm very sorry.
I have a ten year old girl and a six-year-old boy in there.
There's a family counsel-- there's a family counselor on the way.
They're waiting for the daddy to bring them ice cream. What do I tell them?
I'm sorry, Mam.
What do I tell them?
I don't know. Tell me.
Mam, I believe you're in shock.
What do I tell them?
I don't know. Tell me.
Shock.
We're very sorry, the counselor will be able to help you.
It's as if this is the only question in the world to her.
Tell me what to tell them. The Police Officer is tearing up. Jesus... Tell me.
Audrey is not. POLICE OFFICER
Jesus, I'm sorry.
I don't know what to tell them.
His partner steps in... taking over. HIS PARTNER
What do I tell them?
Mrs. Burke, we need to come inside now. Tell me. know.
Tell me because I don't
A counselor is on the way. She's not hearing a word he's saying.
Because I don't know what to tell them.
What do I tell--
It drifts off... she looks straight ahead. It's excruciating. He has to collect himself. Draws in a deep breath, then...
Mrs. Burke... we need to come inside now.
And she vacantly shifts her gaze onto him. Doesn't say anything. Doesn't do anything. She's not there.
Mrs. Burke... we need to come inside.
Another long moment. Until she finally says...
Please, just go...
BURKE HOUSE/KITCHEN - NIGHT
Audrey kneels on the floor. She isn't in her head. She won't remember this part in the morning. And she cleans up the broken glass. Harper walks in. Tears running down her face.
Mom... what's happening?
As Audrey looks up to her daughter. INT.
BURKE HOUSE/LIVING ROOM - DAY

It's the next day. FATHER GARRITY sits next to Audrey who stares blankly ahead. She doesn't cry. Only close family and friends litter the silent room. They are... Brian's sister BRENDA, mid 30s, overweight. She cries on the other side of Father Garrity who comforts her. Audrey's brother, NEAL, late 20s, tattoos, a Teddy Bear behind tough guy looks. He stares at his feet. Doesn't know what to say. Audrey's best friend, DIANE, early 30s, glasses... a cerebral attractiveness she's recently grown into. She arranges food on the table and cleans. Dory plays with his toys quietly on the floor. in the room. Harper isn't Brenda looks up to Audrey with tear-laden eyes.
I don't understand.
And Audrey vacantly heads into the kitchen. Dory looks up. He's just quiet. Wide-eyed and quiet. Diane follows Audrey into the kitchen. INT.
BURKE HOUSE/KITCHEN - DAY
Audrey leans against the wall. Diane finds her side. Audrey... Stares blankly ahead.
Diane holds out three pills in her hand. Take them.
I'm not hysterical. hysterical, Diane?
Diane regards Audrey. Do I look Studies her. Diane's never seen her like this before. No emotion whatsoever. Completely cold.
BURKE HOUSE/BEDROOM - DAY
Harper watches TV. Brian's mother, GINNIE, late 60s, fully clothed, sleeps on the bed. Audrey heads in and sits next to her daughter. Harper looks over to her and doesn't say anything.
You were watching this show last week.
Harper nods.
What's it called?
Drake and Josh.
They watch in silence, until Audrey points to the screen...
Which one is that?
Tears stream down the side of Harper's face.
That's Josh, he's fat and he's always sweating but he's funny. Drake is really cool and plays in a band. And they're like halfbrothers or step brothers or something.
Harper's not crying but tearing.
Tearing while watching and
Grandma Ginnie has been sleeping all day.
That's because she's sedated, sweetie.
I don't know what that word means.
She took pills so she could sleep.
Harper nods. Her tears still streaming down her face.
She lost her son, Harper. There's nothing more painful in the world. And now Harper turns to her mother. Full on.
And now Harper's crying.
More than losing your Dad?
